description: Simple built_with relation — framework depends on language

pages.name: FastAPI
pages.content: FastAPI is a modern, fast web framework for building APIs with Python 3.8+. It is built on top of Starlette and Pydantic. FastAPI is written entirely in Python and requires Python 3.7 or higher.

pages.name: Python
pages.content: Python is a high-level, general-purpose programming language. It emphasises code readability and supports multiple paradigms including procedural, object-oriented, and functional programming.

pages.name: Injection Service
pages.content: The injection service computes context for AI agents before each prompt. It depends on the notebook service for page retrieval and the memory service for history event lookup. It also uses pgvector for semantic similarity search.

pages.name: Notebook Service
pages.content: The notebook service manages wiki pages, relations, and graph traversal. It is the core knowledge store for the application.

pages.name: Memory Service
pages.content: The memory service stores and retrieves history events from agent sessions. It supports FTS and vector search.

pages.name: Rate limit middleware
pages.content: The rate limit middleware is a component of the API gateway layer. It intercepts all incoming requests and applies per-key sliding-window throttling before they reach the route handlers.

pages.name: API gateway layer
pages.content: The API gateway layer sits in front of all service routes. It handles authentication verification, rate limiting, request logging, and CORS headers. It is implemented as FastAPI middleware.

description: supersedes temporal relation — new version replaces old

pages.name: Auth v2 (JWT RS256)
pages.content: The current authentication system uses asymmetric JWT with RS256 signing. This supersedes the previous HMAC-based approach from Auth v1. Introduced in Q1 2024.

pages.name: Auth v1 (HMAC, deprecated)
pages.content: DEPRECATED. The original auth system used symmetric HMAC tokens stored in the database. Replaced by Auth v2 due to scalability limitations and the need for stateless verification.

description: Multiple outgoing relations from one page

pages.name: Sleep agent
pages.content: The sleep agent is a background curation process that runs on a schedule. It depends on the LLM service (Anthropic Claude) to generate summaries and extract relations. It reads from the memory service and writes to the notebook service. It is part of the curation pipeline.

pages.name: LLM service
pages.content: The LLM service wraps the Anthropic API. It handles prompt construction, retry logic, and token counting.

pages.name: Curation pipeline
pages.content: The curation pipeline orchestrates all background knowledge management. It includes the sleep agent, the relation extractor, and the index maintenance jobs.

description: Bidirectional neighbour lookup — both incoming and outgoing

pages.name: Auth0 SDK
pages.content: The Auth0 SDK handles the OAuth2/OIDC flow for human user authentication. It integrates with the Next.js frontend via the proxy.ts middleware.

pages.name: BFF proxy
pages.content: The BFF proxy is a Next.js server-side route handler that sits between the browser and the FastAPI backend. It reads Auth0 sessions and attaches Bearer tokens to upstream requests. It depends on the Auth0 SDK for session management.

description: Confidence threshold — low-confidence relation should not be retrieved at threshold 0.6

pages.name: Monitoring dashboard
pages.content: The monitoring dashboard displays system metrics. It may have some loose connection to the alerting system.

pages.name: Alerting system
pages.content: The alerting system sends PagerDuty notifications when SLO thresholds are breached.

description: Relation invalidation — upsert same triple updates valid_until on old relation

pages.name: Cache layer
pages.content: The cache layer uses Redis for all caching operations. Initially used Memcached but migrated to Redis for richer data structures.

pages.name: Redis
pages.content: Redis is an in-memory data structure store used for caching and pub/sub.

pages.name: Memcached
pages.content: Memcached is a legacy caching layer, no longer in active use.
